open_known_gaps.summary: Client-side memory-exhaustion (PDF-bomb DoS) class — tracked, no server impact (a user can only exhaust their own browser tab). viewer.js loadDocument() (the single LOAD boundary, line 93) still has no numPages>1000 / cumulative-byte cap and no processing timeout: after getDocument(...).promise it emits PDF_LOADED + renders all pages (TASK-316 supersede-race guards present + re-verified this run at viewer.js token checks 123/129/171/189/202, so footprint is linear — one canvas per page — not multiplied). All per-page / whole-doc consumers sit downstream of this one uncapped boundary and are NOT new load boundaries: (1) viewer.js one canvas/page (dpr capped at 2); (2) thumbnails.js a 2nd canvas/page (mitigated by 120px width + renderToken guard); (3) merge.js loads base+each queued file with no cumulative byte/page cap; (4) pages.js/page-numbers.js/metadata.js/bates.js getData()->pdf-lib.load()->save() over the open doc; (5) convert.js renders ONE page to a throwaway canvas with its own MAX=8000px cap; (6) NEW this tick: crop.js (TASK-335) getData()->pdf-lib.load()->getCropBox/setCropBox per page->save() over the already-open doc — same class, operates only on the validated open document, needs no separate cap. Single right fix = developer task: a page-count/byte cap at the viewer.js LOAD boundary protects every downstream consumer at once. Documented in docs/security-guide.md.

audit_notes.crop_js: TASK-335 trim a uniform margin off every page via the PDF crop box. Operates on the already-open doc via currentDoc.getData() -> pdf-lib load/getCropBox/setCropBox/save -> Blob download (NOT a new load boundary). Non-destructive (only shrinks crop box, page content untouched). Per-page effective margin clamped so width/height never drop below MIN_SIDE_PT=1pt (no zero/negative-area crop box); margin parsed via readMarginPt() -> finite, non-negative, capped at MAX_MARGIN_PT=5000. XSS-safe: no user-controlled string reaches innerHTML — only pdf-lib and statusEl.textContent. buildFileName() strips to [a-zA-Z0-9._-], slice(0,180), default 'document' -> <base>_cropped.pdf. Safe blob+createObjectURL+a.download+rel=noopener, anchor removed, URL revoked after 1s. Controls disabled until PDF_LOADED; no-doc Apply shows 'Load a PDF first.' status, never throws; PDFLib-unready and margin<=0 guarded; errors caught + surfaced via EventBus.ERROR.
audit_notes.upload_js_changes: TASK-334 full-window 'Drop PDF to open' overlay. validate() core UNCHANGED: sanitizeName strips path separators (\ /), space, dash -> _ and slice(0,200); requires .pdf extension; rejects MIME!=application/pdf when present; rejects size==0 and size>50MB; hasPdfMagic() checks first 5 bytes == %PDF- (0x25 0x50 0x44 0x46 0x2d). Drag overlay is depth-counted (dragDepth) so no flicker; dragHasFiles() only shows it for 'Files' drags (text/element drags ignored); overlay toggles hidden + aria-hidden, never blocks input. Window drop handler hardened with `e.target instanceof Node` guard before dropZone.contains() (prevents latent crash on non-Node targets). No innerHTML anywhere; status via textContent. No new load boundary or external request introduced.
audit_notes.innerHTML_in_js: only empty-string clears or comments across the whole js/ tree — no user/PDF content sink anywhere
audit_notes.external_fetch_or_xhr: none — no fetch/XMLHttpRequest/sendBeacon/WebSocket and no non-local http(s) URLs in js/ (100% client-side, PDFs never leave the browser)
audit_notes.secrets_scan: git grep for private keys / AWS keys / sk- tokens / inline passwords over tracked tree (excluding *.md and status/*.json) -> no real secret
